sections.paragraph:
constr. שֵׁם, sometimes followed by Makk. שֶׁם־, with suff. שְׁמִי, שִׁמְךָ, שִׁמְכֶם, pl. שֵׁמֹות, constr. שְׁמֹות m. (once f. Son 1:3, see רוּק Hophal).

(1) name. (Arab. إِسْمُ, more rarely سِمُ, Eth. ስም፡ but Ch. שֻׁם id. I regard this word as primitive, and prop. as denoting σῆμα, signum, although kindred to it is the root وسم to mark with a sign, to designate, سِمَةُ sign, stigma, mark with which any one is marked. From the noun are derived سمى Conj. II., ܫܰܡܗܰ, ܫܰܡܺܝ to name. Some regard שֵׁם as shortened from שֵׁמַע, by casting away ע, comparing the LXX. translator, who not unfrequently renders שֵׁמַע, ὄνομα.) בְּשֵׁם פ׳ in any one’s name, i.e. authority, Exo 5:23 Est 3:12 בְּשֵׁם יְיָ in the name of Jehovah, by his authority, Jer 11:21 26:9 בְּשֵׁם by name (ben Namen), Exo 33:12 בְּשֵׁמֹות by name, 1Ch 12:31 Ezr 10:16. As to the phrases קָרָא שֵׁם, נִקְרָא בְשֵׁם etc. see קָרָא No. 2, -fh, No. 3 , and Niphal No. 1, 2. Specially it is-(a) a celebrated name, fame (like ὄνομα and nomen). עָשָׂה לֹו שֵׁם Gen 11:4 Jer 32:20 and שׂוּם לֹו שֵׁם 2Sa 7:23, to make for oneself a name, i.e. to acquire fame for oneself; אַנְשֵׁי שֵׁם famous men, Gen 6:4 also nobles, Num 16:2 אַנְשֵׁי שֵׁמֹות id. 1Ch 5:24 and on the other hand, בְּנֵי בְלִי שֵׁם sons of an ignoble (father), i.e. ignoble themselves, and sprung from an ignoble race, Job 30:8. Hence glory, Gen 9:27, בְּאָהֳלֵי שֵׁם “in glorious tents;” [Is not שֵׁם here pr.n.] Zep 3:19, שַׂמְתִּים לִתְהִלָּה וּלְשֵׁם “I will make them praised and famous;” verse 20 Zep 3:20 Deuteron. 26:19 .-(b) a good name, good reputation, Ecc 7:1 Pro 22:1. When used in a bad sense there is added רַע Deu 22:14, 19 Deu 22:19 Neh 6:13.-(c) fame after death, memory. So in the phrases, to destroy, to blot out the name of any person or thing, i.e. so to blot out (a people, a city), that even the name and memory may perish from posterity, Deu 9:14 1Sa 24:22 2Ki 14:27 Psa 9:6 Zec 13:2 also Ecc 6:4, “its name is covered with darkness” (of an abortion). Hence-(d) a monument, by which any one’s memory is preserved, 2Sa 8:13 Isa 55:13. [This meaning appears to be very doubtful in both the cited passages.]

(2) שֵׁם יְהֹוָה is-(a) the celebrated name of God, the estimation of men concerning God; in the phrase לְמַעַן שְׁמֹו for his name’s sake, as his name would lead one to expect; see page 495, B hence the glory of God; לְמַעַן שְׁמִי for my name’s sake, lest the glory of the divine name should suffer. Isai. 48:9 1Ki 8:41 Psa 79:9 106:8 Eze 20:44. Psa 138:2, עַל כָּל שִׁמְךָ “above all thy name,” above all that can be predicated of thee.-(b) Jehovah, as being called on and praised by men, as קָרָא בְּשֵׁם יְהֹוָה to call on the name of Jehovah, compare under the verb קָרָא No. 1, h. Psa 5:12, אֹהֲבֵי שְׁמֶךָ “those who love thy name,” i.e. those who delight in thy praise. Psa 9:11.-(c) the Deity as being present with mortals, i.q. פְּנֵי יְהֹוָה. Exo 23:21, כִּי שְׁמִי בְּקִרְבֹּו “for my name is in him” (the angel). 1Ki 8:29, יִהְיֶה שְׁמִי שָׁם “my name shall be there” (in the temple). 2Ki 23:27. 1Ki 3:2, “no house had been built to the name of the Lord.” 1Ki 8:17, 20 1Ki 8:20. שִׁכֵּז שְׁמֹו, שׂוּם to put his name (in any place), i.e. there to fix his abode, see under the verb שׂוּם and שָׁכַז . It is often applied to the aid which God as present vouchsafes to men. Psa 54:3, “O God! בְּשִׁמְךָ הֹושִׁיעֵנוּ save us by thy name.” Psa 44:6 124:8 89:25 20:2 Isai. 30:27 . Also שֵׁם, הַשֵּׁם are used absol. of the name of God, Lev 24:11, 16 Lev 24:16 Deu 28:58.

(3) pr.n. Shem, the eldest [second] son of Noah, from whom, -Gen. 10:2230 , the Shemitic nations, i.e. the western nations of Asia, the Persians, Assyrians, Aramean s, and part of the Arabs have sprung. Compare Gesch. der Hebr. Spr. u. Schr. p. 5, 6.

Compound pr.n. are שְׁמוּאֵל, שְׁמִידָע, שְׁמִירָמֹות.
